name: Boston Cream Dessert
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine the egg yolks, water, lemon rind, and vanilla. Add sugar (reserve 1 tablespoon), then blend well.
2. Add lemon juice, flour, salt, and baking powder. Stir until combined.
3. Beat the 3 egg whites with the reserved 1 tablespoon of sugar until stiff peaks form.
4. Gradually fold one-third of the egg whites into the batter, then add the remaining egg whites.
5. Pour the batter evenly into two greased and floured 8-inch layer cake pans lined with wax paper.
6. Bake at 325°F for 35-40 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool completely.
1. To make the filling, heat 1 cup of milk and butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
2. In a separate bowl, combine flour, salt, and sugar.
3. Gradually add 1/2 cup of cold milk to the dry ingredients, stirring until smooth.
4. Slowly pour some of the hot milk into the dry mixture while continuously stirring.
5. Return the mixture to the saucepan and cook over low heat, stirring constantly until thickened.
6. Fill the cooled cake layers with the filling and frost as desired. Garnish with sliced peaches or other fruit dipped in lemon juice and whipped topping, if desired.
